prompt:
A Meridian Training Analytics Management System that includes student and mentor-facing learning tools, operational reporting, and offline-first session capture for on-premise training providers. The Angular web interface supports four roles—Student, Corporate Mentor, Faculty Mentor, and Administrator—each seeing a role-appropriate navigation and data scope. Users register with a username and password and remain in a pending state until an Administrator approves the account within 2 business days; passwords must be at least 12 characters with at least one number and one symbol, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts. Students start a training session with an in-session timer, between-set rest timer (default 60 seconds, adjustable 15–300 seconds), and completion check-offs per activity; the UI provides immediate “saved locally” feedback when connectivity is unavailable and allows resume after interruption with a one-tap “continue session” flow. Mentors and admins access learning and assessment analytics that surface mastery trends, wrong-answer distributions, weak knowledge-point analysis, and item difficulty/discrimination at learner, cohort, and course levels, with interactive filtering by date range, location, instructor, and course version. The Operations & Reporting Center provides configurable business reports for enrollments, seat utilization, refunds/return rate, training-material inventory levels, and upcoming certification expirations (e.g., expiring within 30/60/90 days), with scheduled exports to local CSV/PDF files and in-app notifications using editable templates; exports and dashboards are permission-isolated so corporate mentors can only see their organization’s learners and purchases.

The backend uses Spring Boot to implement a decoupled REST API consumed by the Angular client, with PostgreSQL as the system-of-record for users, roles, courses, assessment items, attempts, session logs, operational transactions, and audit events. Offline resilience is implemented via client-side caching (IndexedDB) for session logs and draft assessments; when the client reconnects to the local server (same device or LAN), a background sync reconciles records using idempotent request keys and last-write-wins rules, while preventing duplicate submissions. Data governance enforces classification tiers (Public/Internal/Confidential/Restricted) and masks sensitive fields by default (e.g., showing only last 4 characters of employee IDs and partial contact details) unless the viewer has explicit permission. Sensitive values are encrypted at rest using AES-256, passwords are salted and hashed with BCrypt, HTTPS is enabled with a locally managed certificate, and standard security headers are enforced. End-to-end audit logging captures logins, exports, permission changes, and data deletions; anomaly alerts are raised as in-app notifications for unusual patterns such as logins from a new device fingerprint, access outside allowed IP ranges, or more than 20 export attempts in 10 minutes, and APIs apply token-based access, configurable rate limits, and approval workflow for permission changes and data exports. Backup and disaster recovery run fully offline: nightly incremental and weekly full PostgreSQL backups to an administrator-defined local path, 30-day retention by default, quarterly recovery drills with recorded results, an optional 14-day recycle bin for accidental deletions, and a documented failover procedure to a standby server on the same network.
